g.title: cluster_6
g.text: New issues surfaced by the npj DM lineage reframe

a.xlink:title: Disclosure 2.0 is the natural extension of the clinical-AI transparency lineage to agentic-LLM research; the published unit becomes a six-item manifest (prompts, model identifier, commit history, reviewer-subagent transcripts, audit log, tagged release) at a tagged DOI release.
a.text: release) at a tagged DOI release.

a.xlink:title: npj Digital Medicine should pilot Disclosure 2.0 as an alternative submission track for AI-deep manuscripts; 5-10 submissions/quarter, 90 days, no Editorial Manager feature work needed.
Final synthesis
Model Facts Labels (Sendak et al. 2020) name the disclosure unit for a deployed clinical ML model.
a.text: name the disclosure unit for a

a.xlink:title: A five-year sequence of clinical-AI transparency standards establishes the disclosure-unit pattern; Disclosure 2.0 completes the sequence for the agentic-LLM class.
The 2020 CONSORT-AI / SPIRIT-AI extensions name it for AI-intervention RCTs.
TRIPOD+AI (Collins et al. 2024) names it for AI-enabled prediction-model reporting.
Each standard atomises an artefact type (deployed model, trial, prediction model) and binds journal-side adoption to author-side compliance.
a.text: Each standard atomises an artefact type

a.xlink:title: Co-Scientist (Nature 2026) autonomously generates and refines biomedical hypotheses; AML drug-repurposing candidates were in vitro validated.
The lineage's standards assume the disclosed unit is a model or a trial; agentic LLMs produce neither.
a.text: disclosed unit is a model or a trial;

a.xlink:title: Sakana AI Scientist (Nature 2026) produced a manuscript that passed first-round blind workshop peer review.
Neither system produces a fixed predictive model with stable weights, nor a clinical trial.
Current biomedical-journal AI policies prohibit-by-default and ask for a single-sentence acknowledgement; prohibition without an audit surface invites camouflage.
MIT-licensed repo at tag viewpoint-npjdm-v1.0.0; every manifest item present.
The present Perspective is itself produced under Disclosure 2.0; the case study returned a preregistered NULL and the Layer-2 audit filed 12 substantive findings on the case-study manuscript.
a.text: NULL and the Layer-2 audit filed 12

a.xlink:title: Layer 3 delta-C-index = 0.006 [-0.008, 0.030], primary hypothesis not rejected, n=333.
Layer 2 filed 1 blocker + 2 high + 9 medium-low findings; within-Layer-1 reviewer rounds did not catch them.
a.text: reviewer rounds did not catch them.

a.xlink:title: A standard that surfaces its own failure modes is the strongest available demonstration.
a.text: A standard that surfaces its own

a.xlink:title: Disclosure 2.0 does not eliminate failure modes — only makes them findable; single case study is not scale evidence.
a.text: findable; single case study is not

a.xlink:title: PDF manifest alongside cover letter + linked Zenodo DOI; editorial office validates against JSON schema.
Pilot success is whether the manifest is CONSULTED by reviewers, not just published.
a.text: Pilot success is whether the manifest
is CONSULTED by reviewers, not just

a.xlink:title: An npj DM editorial board can run the pilot at the cost of one quarter, no software development, and full backward compatibility with the current "readability and language improvements only" lane.
a.text: An npj DM editorial board can run the
pilot at the cost of one quarter, no

a.xlink:title: LLMs are not bit-deterministic; the prompt is not a protocol.
A single operator cannot audit himself.
a.text: A single operator cannot audit himself.

a.xlink:title: Reviewer subagents hallucinate the way the pipeline does.
This proposal is for well-resourced submitters only — clinicians without git fluency or institutional compute cannot meet the full manifest.
The audit subagent runs the same model class as Layer 1; Layer 2 hallucinates too.
a.text: The audit subagent runs the same model

a.xlink:title: Same prompt + model id + snapshot + tool envelope must yield the same selected sub-claim and statistical method across three runs; the headline effect size within the original's bootstrap 95% interval.
a.text: tool envelope must yield the same
method across three runs; the headline

a.xlink:title: Layer 2 is dispatched as a sealed audit subagent; Layer 3 is preregistered before Layer 1 sees the data; reviewer personas are diversified; repository is open to third-party re-audit; seal is testimonial rather than cryptographic.
a.text: Layer 2 is dispatched as a sealed audit
before Layer 1 sees the data; reviewer
is open to third-party re-audit; seal

a.xlink:title: The hallucination is preserved verbatim and the resolution lives in subsequent commits, making the failure mode catchable rather than camouflaged.
a.text: and the resolution lives in subsequent

a.xlink:title: Items 1, 2, 3, 6 of the manifest preserve the consequential audit surfaces at near-zero infrastructure cost.
Layer 1 on one vendor + Layer 2 on a different vendor is the proposed mitigation; the manifest's models array supports declaration; same-vendor audit treated as single-rater study.
a.text: Layer 1 on one vendor + Layer 2 on a

a.xlink:title: The lineage selects Sendak 2020, CONSORT-AI 2020 and TRIPOD+AI 2024 but skips CLAIM 2024, DECIDE-AI 2022, STANDING Together 2024, and the WAME / ICMJE 2024 AI guidance.

Disclosure 2.0 items are claimed to be "analogous to" Model Facts Labels fields, but Model Facts Labels disclose deployment-time properties whereas Disclosure 2.0 discloses process-time properties — different temporal locus.
a.text: Disclosure 2.0 items are claimed to be

a.xlink:title: Mitchell et al. 2019 "Model Cards for Model Reporting" (FAccT) preceded Sendak 2020 by a year and is the actual mother paper of the lineage; not citing it weakens the historical claim.
a.text: Sendak 2020 by a year and is the
actual mother paper of the lineage;
not citing it weakens the historical

a.xlink:title: Three standards are cited because each names a distinct artefact type (deployed model, trial, prediction model); the omitted standards are either imaging-modality-specific (CLAIM), decision-support-specific (DECIDE-AI), reporting-equity-specific (STANDING Together), or generic publication-ethics rather than artefact-disclosure (WAME / ICMJE). The Perspective could add a one-sentence rationale.
a.text: Three standards are cited because each

a.xlink:title: The temporal-locus mismatch IS the gap the Perspective identifies; the lineage's standards cover model-at-deployment and trial-at-design, but no standard covers process-as-it-runs. Disclosure 2.0 fills that locus.
a.text: The temporal-locus mismatch IS the gap

a.xlink:title: Mitchell 2019 was a general ML framework; Sendak 2020 operationalised it for clinical deployment with a one-page Model Facts Label tied to a real implementation. The Perspective could mention Mitchell 2019 as a precursor in one phrase.
a.text: it for clinical deployment with a
one-page Model Facts Label tied to a
